metadata.name: Legitimate Email Dataset
metadata.description: Curated legitimate (ham) email samples representing benign corporate and personal email communications. Derived from the ham portions of well-known public corpora.

sources.name: SpamAssassin Ham Corpus
sources.description: Legitimate (ham) emails from the SpamAssassin public corpus. Includes newsletters, mailing lists, and business correspondence. Used as the negative class in email classification research.

sources.name: Enron Email Dataset (Ham subset)
sources.description: Legitimate emails from the Enron email dataset, covering 150 senior employees' inboxes over several years. One of the largest publicly available email corpora.
sources.citation: Klimt, B. & Yang, Y. (2004). Introducing the Enron Corpus. CEAS 2004.

sources.name: Ling-Spam Corpus (Ham)
sources.description: Ham emails from the Ling-Spam corpus – messages from a linguistics mailing list. Used in early spam research.
sources.citation: Androutsopoulos, I. et al. (2000). An evaluation of naive Bayesian anti-spam filtering. ECML 2000.

feature_vocabulary.description: Top terms associated with legitimate email, used to calibrate the detection engine. Low phishing-indicator frequency.

samples.subject: Q3 results presentation – Thursday 2pm
samples.body:
Hi team,

Just a reminder that our Q3 results presentation is scheduled for Thursday at 2pm in Conference Room B.

Please bring your department updates. I've attached the agenda for your reference.

Let me know if you have any questions.

Best,
Sarah
samples.label: legitimate

samples.subject: Re: Project Phoenix – updated timeline
samples.body:
Hi David,

Following our call yesterday, I've updated the project timeline. The key milestones are:

– Discovery phase: 17-28 March
– Build phase: 1-25 April
– UAT: 28 April – 9 May
– Go-live: 12 May

Let me know if this works from your side.

Cheers,
James
samples.label: legitimate

samples.subject: Laptop issue – IT support ticket #48291
samples.body:
Hi Mark,

Thank you for raising a ticket regarding your laptop. We have scheduled an engineer to visit your desk on Wednesday 15 March at 10am.

Ticket reference: #48291

If you need to reschedule, please reply to this email or call extension 4291.

Kind regards,
IT Helpdesk
samples.label: legitimate

samples.subject: Monthly newsletter – March 2026
samples.body:
Tech Weekly – March 2026

In this issue:
– AI in 2026: what businesses need to know
– Cybersecurity trends: phishing on the rise
– Product review: Windows 12
– Events: Tech Summit London, April 2026

To read the full articles, visit our website at techweekly.co.uk.

To unsubscribe, reply with STOP.

The Tech Weekly Team

Hi everyone,

Please save the date for our annual team away day on Monday 21 April.

samples.subject: Re: Feedback on your draft proposal
samples.body:
Hi Tom,

Thanks for sending over the draft proposal. Overall it's looking strong. A few comments:

1. Section 3 (budget) – the figures look slightly conservative. Can we revisit?
2. Section 5 (timeline) – the Q3 deadline may be ambitious given resourcing.
3. Executive summary – very clear, good work.

Happy to jump on a call to discuss. Available Thursday afternoon?

Best,
Linda
samples.label: legitimate

samples.subject: Password will expire in 14 days – please reset
samples.body:
Hi,

This is an automated reminder that your corporate password will expire in 14 days.

To reset your password, please visit the IT Self-Service Portal at https://selfservice.acmecorp.com and log in with your current credentials.

For help, contact the IT Helpdesk on extension 4200.

IT Operations
samples.label: legitimate
legitimacy_signals: Links to internal portal on same corporate domain

samples.subject: Board meeting minutes – 5 March 2026

samples.subject: Welcome to the team – onboarding information
samples.body:
Hi Emily,

Welcome to Acme Corp! We're delighted to have you joining us on Monday 17 March.

Here's what to expect on your first day:
– Arrive at reception by 9am, ask for James from HR
– IT will set up your laptop and credentials
– Team lunch at 12:30pm

Your employment contract and starter forms are on the HR portal at hr.acmecorp.com.

Looking forward to meeting you!

HR Team
samples.label: legitimate
legitimacy_signals: Internal portal link on same domain

samples.subject: Research paper review – deadline reminder
samples.body:
Dear Reviewer,

This is a reminder that reviews for ACL 2026 are due on 25 March 2026.

Please log in to the Softconf review system at softconf.com/acl2026 to complete your assigned reviews.

If you are unable to review any of your assigned papers, please let us know as soon as possible.

ACL 2026 Programme Committee
samples.label: legitimate
legitimacy_signals: No credential requests outside named system

samples.subject: Action required: complete your annual compliance training
samples.body:
Hi,

As part of our annual compliance programme, all employees are required to complete the following training modules by 31 March:

1. Data Protection & GDPR (30 mins)
2. Anti-Bribery & Corruption (20 mins)
3. Cyber Security Awareness (25 mins)

Access the training portal at learning.acmecorp.com/compliance.

For assistance, contact compliance@acmecorp.com.

Compliance Team
samples.label: legitimate
legitimacy_signals: Internal domain and training portal on same domain

samples.subject: Client proposal – ready for your review
samples.body:
Hi Sarah,

The client proposal for the Henderson account is ready for your review. I've uploaded it to the shared drive at files.acmecorp.com/proposals/henderson-2026.

Key points:
– Three service tiers proposed (Basic, Standard, Premium)
– Pricing modelled on 2024 rates + 5% uplift

Please share any feedback by Wednesday.

Thanks,
David
